ops.text: `ccx vcs ship` in a shared jj checkout parked on a foreign bookmark silently advanced and pushed that bookmark instead of main: in captain-hook, @ sat on the `yasyf/lag-probe-ch` stack, and ship committed my settings change there, advanced the bookmark across several concurrent-session commits (8788c260..879ba5b7), and pushed the lot to origin as that branch — reporting only "pushed yasyf/lag-probe-ch → origin · CI no-run". Recovery took a manual `jj bookmark set --allow-backwards` + push to rewind the foreign branch, then `jj rebase -r <commit> -d main` + bookmark move + push. Ship offers no `--bookmark` flag and no guard; it should refuse (or at least prompt) when the bookmark it is about to advance is not main/trunk, since in cc-pool shared working copies the checkout position routinely belongs to someone else.
